behavior_summary: WALLSWIT::look: When the player examines the wall switch item it displays the label "switch " by calling Item::bark. This is a simple examine/description handler.

WALLSWIT::use: When the player uses the wall switch the script reads the item's graphic frame and a 'quality' mode field, updates the frame to represent a toggled/stepped switch position, plays feedback SFX, and spawns short helper routines (FREE) to stage multi-step visual changes or timing.

Quality acts as a mode selector: quality==0/1 behaves as a binary invert toggle (calls Camera::invertScreen and flips the stored quality between 0 and 1); quality values >=2 enter configuration branches that iterate data tables and spawn slider UI processes (SLIDER::0080 or SLIDER::01A1) to let the player configure related values.
Frame values drive whether use increments or decrements the visual frame (distinct branches for frames like 0,1,2,3,5 observed) and determine which slider-table entries match for spawning sliders.
No global flags are read or written by this script; behavior is driven by item-local fields (frame and quality) and transient local/process state.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: U8P_WALLSWIT::look is bound to Event 0 (U8P_WALLSWIT::look). U8P_WALLSWIT::use is the Event 1 (use) handler that runs on player interaction.

Use handler first reads item frame (BP-06h) and item quality (BP-08h), then selects a branch: for certain frames (e.g., 0 or 3) it increments the frame; for other frames (e.g., 2 or 5) it decrements the frame. Each frame change sequence plays a SFX and spawns FREE::1C19 for timed/multi-step behavior.

After changing frame, the handler inspects quality: if quality == 0 or 1 it toggles Camera::invertScreen and flips the stored quality (0 <-> 1) while playing an SFX; if quality >= 2 it enters table-driven loops that compare entries to the frame/quality and spawn slider UIs (SLIDER::0080 or SLIDER::01A1).
Table-driven slider branches iterate a pointer/index (BP-04h) and use a small byte (BP-09h) as a spawn flag to track whether a slider was launched.
open_questions: Exact runtime behavior of the spawned FREE::1C19 routine: inferred to be a short animation/timer helper but its concrete effects are not present in this script.
Precise meanings of the numeric frame values (which frame numbers correspond to 'on' vs 'off' or intermediate positions) and full mapping of all possible frame states.
Exact structure and content of the slider parameter tables referenced in the quality>=2 branches (what values are being configured and how they map to game systems).
Whether any external game state beyond item-local fields (e.g., world-level visual effects) is affected by the slider spawns or FREE routine (not visible in this script).

main_function.name: U8P_WALLSWIT::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player 'U8P_WALLSWIT::look' (examine) event for the wall switch item by emitting a short descriptive text ('switch ') via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_WALLSWIT::look) — triggered when the player or actor inspects the switch instance

function_map.name: U8P_WALLSWIT::look
function_map.role: Examine/description handler that calls Item::bark to present the item's label.

function_map.name: U8P_WALLSWIT::use
function_map.role: Use/interaction handler: toggles the switch visual frame, plays SFX, may spawn a FREE helper for timing/animation, and dispatches slider UI spawns when in configuration modes (quality>=2).

external_calls.purpose: Display the item's descriptive string ('switch ') to the examining process/player.
Read the item's current graphic frame/state to decide whether to increment or decrement and to match slider-table entries.
Write the item's new graphic frame/state to reflect toggled/stepped positions after use.
Read the item's 'quality' field used as a mode selector (invert toggle vs slider/config modes).
Update the quality field when toggling invert modes (flip between 0 and 1).
Play auditory feedback for frame toggles and mode toggles (different SFX ids observed).
Spawn a short/timed helper routine after changing frame—likely to stage multi-step animation or a delayed effect.
Invert or restore screen/camera visual effect when flipping the quality invert mode (quality 0/1).
Launch a slider UI/process to configure a numeric value when the switch is in a slider/configuration mode (quality branch, table-driven spawn).
Alternate slider spawn used from a different configuration table branch — launches a different slider UI instance.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: WALLSWIT::look: When the player examines the wall switch item it displays the label "switch " by calling Item::bark. This is a simple examine/description handler.
